title: Solar EUV/X-ray Image Reconstruction
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Solar EUV/X-ray Image Reconstruction: Solar EUV/X-ray DEM inversion: recover differential emission measure DEM(T) from multi-channel EUV images. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (K.psf.coded_aperture…); recovery is posed as a linear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~80; calibration_uncertainty_percent, temperature_response_error set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: I_c = integral K_c(T) * DEM(T) dT + noise_c, for each channel c
E.world_state_x: DEM(T) in R^{N_T x H x W}: emission measure per temperature bin per pixel
E.observation_y: multi-channel EUV/X-ray images I_c in R^{H x W}, c=1..N_channels

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D_emission_measure is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 80); calibration_uncertainty_percent dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Shot poisson sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: chi2 <= 1.5 for well-calibrated channels; DEM uncertainty factor <= 3. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~80. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |calibration_uncertainty_percent|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_T * N_channels * H * W) per inversion per iteration
